title: Anacyclus aureus
taxonomy: Anacyclus aureus is classified as a species within the genus Anacyclus and the family Asteraceae. The genus Anacyclus is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region and the Middle East. It belongs to the order Asterales and is part of the clade Pan-asterids. This classification places Anacyclus aureus in the larger group of flowering plants called Angiosperms. The species is further classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Anacyclus aureus, also known as the yellow chamomile or golden chamomile, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and nausea. Additionally, Anacyclus aureus is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it a potential treatment for anxiety and insomnia. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

constituents: Anacyclus aureus contains various active constituents, including sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. parts: Anacyclus aureus is commonly known as the yellow chamomile or golden chamomile. Anacyclus aureus flowers and roots are used in traditional medicine. side_effects: Anacyclus aureus is known to cause various side effects, including gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its potential toxicity. The plant's essential oils may also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Additionally, Anacyclus aureus may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions like diabetes and high blood pressure. Its use is not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential risks to the developing fetus or infant. More research is needed to fully understand the side effects and risks associated with using Anacyclus aureus.
intro: Anacyclus aureus, also known as golden chamomile or yellow chamomile, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Anacycus aureus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as spilanthol and chamazulene, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Anacycus aureus is used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
